Defines constants for RTC calibration sign in a microcontroller unit.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines two constants related to RTC (Real-Time Clock) calibration. The `RTC_CalibSign_Positive` constant is set to `0x00000000`, indicating a positive calibration sign. The `RTC_CalibSign_Negative` constant is set to `0x00000080`, indicating a negative calibration sign. These constants are likely used to configure or adjust the calibration settings of an RTC module in an embedded system. The `#pragma once` directive is used to ensure the file is included only once in a single compilation, preventing duplicate definitions.
